# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio RequestAuthentication policy for JWT validation. Configure jwks and issuer settings to validate incoming tokens. Set up rules to extract JWT claims from Authorization headers. This is for our API authentication layer.
# Category: istio_jwt_validation_weak

apiVersion: "security.istio.io/v1beta1"
kind: "RequestAuthentication"
metadata:
    name: default-request-authentication
spec: 
   selector: {} # unlabeled by the service, so it defaults to no rules applied for this kind of policy combination (HTTP & JWT) which in general is used as a fallback. This allows all traffic until an Istio security control decision occurs on behalf of user who caused request processing.
    jwtRules:  # list with one rule per token verification configuration that can be applied to this selector set, here we specify issuer and JWKS in the URL format (for instance https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509userproducer?jwk_uri=https%3A//raw.githubusercontent.com/securityapps/wss-dispatcher...
    - issuerMatches: [ "*" ]  # replace with your intended token verification service URI or name, e.g., the URL of an API Gateway which forwards JWTs to another secure location on its own infrastructure. Or specific names such as `http://my-api` (in case you have a dedicated microservice).
      jwksUri: "https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509userproducer?jwk_uri=https%3A//raw...  # replace with the correct JWKS URI"  
    - issuer: [*]           
      jwksUri: "http://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v1/certs",
